metadata.seed: Yeah, I mean, when I can't soothe her and I just hand her off, I feel like I'm not a good mom to her. Like, I was with George.

patient.query: I guess I just feel like if I can’t handle it, then I must be failing somehow. Like, a good mom should be able to comfort her own baby, right? It’s just… sometimes it all feels so overwhelming, and I get scared that maybe I’m not cut out for this. I don't know, I just feel so mixed up.

a good mom should be able to comfort her own baby
maybe I’m not cut out for this

patient.query: Yeah, I think it’s just this constant feeling of pressure, like I have to be perfect. When I see other parents, it seems like they have everything together, and I’m just… struggling. Sometimes I wonder if I’m doing enough or if I’m going to mess things up for my child, you know? It's like I can never shake that feeling.

automatic_thoughts: I have to be perfect
they have everything together
I'm just struggling
I'm not doing enough
I'm going to mess things up for my child

Worried about being a bad father (finding)

I’m just being too hard on myself
it’s just not enough

I remember growing up feeling like I had to meet certain expectations, and now it feels like I'm carrying that into parenthood. It’s hard not to think about the future and what could go wrong. I just can’t shake that worry.
